# UC-22.19.17 · Privileged and remote access — indicator 2

## Description

Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

## Value

Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP by operationalizing "Privileged and remote access — indicator 2" across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=pam`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:pta earliest=-30d safe="Fed-Admin"
| eval uc_tag="22.19.17"
| stats dc(user) as checkouts by target_account, month=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m")
| where checkouts>400
```

Understanding this SPL

**CyberArk Fed-Admin safe checkout surge (FISMA / FedRAMP)** — Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

Documented **Data sources**: CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: pam; **sourcetype**: cyberark:pta. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=pam, sourcetype=cyberark:pta, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by target_account, month=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m")** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where checkouts>400` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the FedRAMP ConMon scan results and the agency or provider evidence pack for the same time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map

## SPL

```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:pta earliest=-30d safe="Fed-Admin"
| eval uc_tag="22.19.17"
| stats dc(user) as checkouts by target_account, month=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m")
| where checkouts>400
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map. Key fields: `%Y-%m")`, `checkouts`, `month=strftime(_time`, `target_account`, `uc_tag`.
